title: 2 hospitalized after head-on collision on Cape Cod
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Two drivers — one from North Carolina and the other from Pocasset — were injured in the collision, police say.
A red 2004 Jeep and a 2023 Toyota pick-up truck collided in the west-bound travel lane of Sandwich Road Wednesday morning.
Bourne Police Department A man was flown to a hospital by Boston MedFlight and another was transported by ambulance following a head-on crash in Bourne on Wednesday morning, authorities said.

title: Map: Boston unveils list of 175+ public bathrooms and other public resources
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Local News The new map spans nearly every corner of Boston.
A pedestrian passes the public restroom near City Hall.

(Lane Turner/Globe Staff) By Annie Jonas June 17, 2026 | 4:16 PM 1 minute to read In the midst of a busy summer tourism season, Boston officials have unveiled an expanded public amenities map that aims to make the city easier to navigate for residents, workers, and visitors alike.

title: An approachable World Cup guide: Ghana
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: World Cup Ghana will face England in Foxborough on June 23.
Ghana's Antoine Semenyo prior to a pre-World Cup friendly with Wales.

Ben STANSALL / AFP via Getty Images By Hayden Bird June 17, 2026 | 4:02 PM 2 minutes to read Ghana will be one of the last teams in the field of 48 to make its 2026 World Cup debut, kicking off on June 17 against Panama in Toronto.

title: Healey announces statewide initiative to decrease wrong-way driving
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News New technologies will warn potential wrong-way drivers before they make a mistake, and problem intersections are being targeted for infrastructure improvements.
Massachusetts officials are rolling out a series of infrastructure improvements to crack down on wrong-way driving.

title: These Massachusetts beaches are closed as of June 17
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Local News Heading to the beach?
Check the state's water quality dashboard to make sure it's open.

title: Brighton man found ‘badly decomposed’ after assisted living didn’t check on him for days, family says
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News “Felipe Dip’s body had been decomposing in his room, undiscovered, for approximately nine days,” the family alleged in a new lawsuit.

By Abby Patkin June 17, 2026 | 3:23 PM 1 minute to read To Felipe Dip’s family, the red flags were obvious: The 64-year-old had missed at least 27 meals straight at his assisted living facility in Brighton, and it had been more than a week since a motion-sensing camera in his bedroom detected any movement.
And yet, according to Dip’s family, no one working at Chestnut Park at Cleveland Circle “conducted a welfare check, entered Felipe Dip’s room, or otherwise investigated his whereabouts or well-being.

title: Fan who traveled to Boston from Scotland for World Cup dies suddenly before getting to see match
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News “Since Scotland qualified in November, he’s never shut up about it.
He was telling everybody this was his lifelong dream.
” Scotland supporters embrace and celebrate after Scotland defeated Haiti, 1-0, in their Group C opener Saturday night in Foxborough.

title: Former Celtic Al Horford’s Brookline home lists for $15 million
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: Luxury Homes The Golden State Warrior is parting ways with his Brookline mansion.
The house at 33 Sears Road in Brookline belongs to former Celtic Al Horford.

title: How do you build a barbecue empire in Boston?
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Food News Andy Husbands spent the last decade turning The Smoke Shop from a Kendall Square startup into a six-location business.
The Smoke Shop is celebrating its 10th anniversary, marking a decade of growth from a single Kendall Square restaurant to six locations across Massachusetts.

title: Boston hotels are adopting ships for Sail Boston 2026
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Local News Hotels across the city are adjusting their programming and menus to honor the countries where the ships hail from.
Adirondack III from Boston in front of Boston Harbor Hotel during a previous tall ship celebration.
Boston Harbor Hotel City hotels are busy designing decor, food, and cocktails that compliment the countries of the tall ships visiting Boston next month for Sail Boston 2026.
